commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the runtime wildcard versus static profile precedence before an automated repair should rewrite this boundary.; Needs attention: The diff widens a tool-access boundary by letting runtime wildcard allowlists bypass static profile caps across shared agent tool construction.; Review finding: [P1] Do not bypass profile caps for every wildcard allowlist ÔÇö src/agents/agent-tools.ts:626-63... (sha=f91b77b1e9af267908ca1d10db501848871dadf6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR skips `tools.profile` and `tools.byProvider.profile` filtering when `runtimeToolAllowlist` contains `*`, then adds focused agent and cron tests for wildcard allowlists with the messaging profile.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +122. Total +134 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main carries cron `toolsAllow` into `runtimeToolAllowlist` but still applies the messaging profile before the final runtime allowlist filter. I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the runtime wildcard versus static profile precedence before an automated repair should rewrite this boundary.

target.title: fix(agents): allow runtime wildcard tool allowlist to override staticÔÇĞ
target.body:
## Summary

When a cron job sets `toolsAllow: ["*"]`, the runtime wildcard should override the agent's static tool profile (e.g. `messaging`) so that `exec` and other coding tools remain available ÔÇö including background/yield behavior.

Previously, `createOpenClawCodingTools` applied the static profile policy via `applyToolPolicyPipeline` before considering the runtime allowlist. This caused `exec` to be filtered out for cron sessions using the `messaging` profile even when `toolsAllow: ["*"]` was explicitly set. Additionally, `allowBackground` was computed from the original profile before the wildcard override, causing exec to become visible while `background: true` still ran synchronously.

### Key Changes

- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`: When `runtimeToolAllowlist` contains `*`, skip `profilePolicy` and `providerProfilePolicy` steps in both `allowBackground` derivation (moved before allowBackground computation per review feedback) and the final tool policy pipeline.
- `src/agents/agent-tools.runtime-allowlist-override.test.ts`: Regression tests for `createOpenClawCodingTools` with wildcard + messaging profile.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts`: Integration test simulating the full cron isolated session tool construction path through `resolveEmbeddedAttemptToolConstructionPlan` and `createOpenClawCodingTools`.

### Issue Reference

Fixes #89228 (Bug B: exec unavailable in isolated sessions)

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: `exec` tool is now available with correct background behavior when cron isolated sessions use `toolsAllow: ["*"]` with a `messaging` profile agent.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.22, real OpenClaw checkout at `07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw`, branch `fix/cron-exec-wildcard-override-89228`, commit `f91b77b1e`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/agent-tools.runtime-allowlist-override.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts \
  --run --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
 RUN  v4.1.7

 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts > cron isolated session tool construction with messaging profile > wildcard allowlist overrides messaging profile for tool construction plan 4ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts > cron isolated session tool construction with messaging profile > messaging profile without wildcard excludes shell tools from plan 1ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts > cron isolated session tool construction with messaging profile > createOpenClawCodingTools includes exec with wildcard + messaging profile 1082ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/agent-tools.runtime-allowlist-override.test.ts > createOpenClawCodingTools runtimeToolAllowlist override > includes exec when runtimeToolAllowlist contains wildcard even with messaging profile 1118ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts > cron isolated session tool construction with messaging profile > createOpenClawCodingTools excludes exec with messaging profile only 74ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/cron-isolated-session-tools.test.ts > cron isolated session tool construction with messaging profile > allowBackground is consistent with exec visibility under wildcard + messaging 19ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/agent-tools.runtime-allowlist-override.test.ts > createOpenClawCodingTools runtimeToolAllowlist override > excludes exec with messaging profile and no runtime wildcard 88ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/agent-tools.runtime-allowlist-override.test.ts > createOpenClawCodingTools runtimeToolAllowlist override > allows exec background when runtimeToolAllowlist contains wildcard with messaging profile 24ms
 Ô£ô unit-fast src/agents/agent-tools.runtime-allowlist-override.test.ts > createOpenClawCodingTools runtimeToolAllowlist override > does not allow exec background with messaging profile and no runtime wildcard 14ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
   Start at  16:42:24
   Duration  45.35s
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- `toolsAllow: ["*"]` + `messaging` profile ÔåÆ `exec` is present in tool list Ô£ô
- `toolsAllow: ["*"]` + `messaging` profile ÔåÆ `allowBackground` is not false (consistent with exec visibility) Ô£ô
- `messaging` profile without wildcard ÔåÆ `exec` correctly absent (narrow profile preserved) Ô£ô
- Tool construction plan correctly includes `includeShellTools: true` under wildcard Ô£ô
- All 9 tests pass in 45.35s on real checkout Ô£ô

### Live Cron Scheduler Proof

**Behavior addressed**: `shell-tools` stage (including `exec`) now passes through tool policy pipeline when cron isolated sessions use `toolsAllow: ["*"]` with a `messaging` profile agent.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.22, local build from branch `analyze/cron-exec-89228` commit `f91b77b1e`, gateway started via `node dist/index.js gateway`.

**Exact steps**:
1. Build from PR branch: `pnpm build`
2. Set `toolsAllow: ["*"]` on a cron job with `sessionTarget: "isolated"` (messaging profile agent)
3. Start local gateway: `node dist/index.js gateway`
4. Run cron job: `openclaw cron run <job-id> --wait --expect-final --timeout 120000`

**Evidence ÔÇö gateway log trace after fix** (redacted):
```
[trace:embedded-run] core-plugin-tool stages:
  runId=dreaming-narrative-light-...
  sessionId=a88a94d5-...
  phase=core-plugin-tools totalMs=33234
  stages=tool-policy:54ms@54ms,
         workspace-policy:1ms@55ms,
         base-coding-tools:2ms@57ms,
         shell-tools:2ms@59ms,          ÔåÉ exec passes through
         openclaw-tools:session-workspace:2ms@61ms,
         ...
         authorization-policy:2ms@33221ms,
         schema-normalization:11ms@33232ms
```

Cron run result:
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "durationMs": 79002,
  "jobName": "Memory Dreaming Promotion",
  "model": "Qwen3-235B-A22B",
  "sessionId": "38e48c9b-db4e-4ba8-b15e-9523d456beea"
}
```

**Key observation**: `shell-tools:2ms@59ms` appears in the tool construction pipeline ÔÇö the messaging profile did not filter out shell/exec tools when `toolsAllow: ["*"]` was set. Without this patch, `shell-tools` would be absent from the pipeline for messaging-profile agents.

**Observed result after fix**:
- Vitest: 9/9 tests pass Ô£ô
- Live cron: `shell-tools` stage present in embedded-run trace Ô£ô
- Live cron: job completes `status: "ok"` Ô£ô
- `messaging` profile without wildcard ÔåÆ `exec` correctly absent (narrow profile preserved) Ô£ô

### Security Boundary Note

ClawSweeper raised a P1 concern: wildcard `runtimeToolAllowlist: ["*"]` bypasses profile caps for every embedded caller, not just cron. This is intentional ÔÇö `runtimeToolAllowlist` is an operator-set field that explicitly widens tool availability. Cron is the primary use case (`resolveEmbeddedAttemptToolConstructionPlan` sets it from `payload.toolsAllow`), but any embedded caller that the operator configures with `toolsAllow: ["*"]` should honor that widening. This changes the precedence: runtime operator intent > static profile defaults. Maintainers should decide whether to accept this precedence rule or narrow it to a cron-specific path.
target.author: tanshanshan

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
